template: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of living things?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 160" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Characteristics of Living Things</text>
  <rect x="20" y="28" width="80" height="50" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="60" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Need food</text>
  <text x="60" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ Living</text>
  <rect x="110" y="28" width="80" height="50" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="150" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Can grow</text>
  <text x="150" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ Living</text>
  <rect x="200" y="28" width="80" height="50" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="240" y="44" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Make own</text>
  <text x="240" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">light</text>
  <text x="240" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ NOT living</text>
  <rect x="290" y="28" width="80" height="50" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="330" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Reproduce</text>
  <text x="330" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ Living</text>
  <rect x="30" y="90" width="310" height="28" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="185" y="108" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">Other living characteristics: move, respond, respire, excrete</text>
  <text x="190" y="142" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#dc2626" font-weight="bold">Answer: "Make their own light" is NOT a characteristic ★</text>
</svg>
options: They can make their own light
correct_answer: They can make their own light

template: {CHARACTER_0} is studying a butterfly that goes through complete metamorphosis. What is the correct order of its life cycle?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 160" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Butterfly Life Cycle (Complete Metamorphosis)</text>
  <defs><marker id="arr7" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6 Z" fill="#7c3aed"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="15" y="45" width="70" height="45" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="50" y="63" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1e40af">1. Egg</text>
  <text x="50" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#475569">laid on leaf</text>
  <line x1="85" y1="67" x2="102" y2="67" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr7)"/>
  <rect x="103" y="45" width="70" height="45" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="138" y="63" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">2. Larva</text>
  <text x="138" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#475569">(caterpillar)</text>
  <line x1="173" y1="67" x2="190" y2="67" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr7)"/>
  <rect x="191" y="45" width="70" height="45" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="226" y="63" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">3. Pupa</text>
  <text x="226" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#475569">(chrysalis)</text>
  <line x1="261" y1="67" x2="278" y2="67" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr7)"/>
  <rect x="279" y="45" width="80" height="45" fill="#ede9fe" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="319" y="63" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#7c3aed">4. Adult ★</text>
  <text x="319" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#475569">(butterfly)</text>
  <text x="190" y="125" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#1e293b" font-weight="bold">Correct order: Egg → Larva → Pupa → Adult</text>
  <text x="190" y="142" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Complete metamorphosis = 4 stages (very different body forms)</text>
</svg>

template: Which part of a plant makes food for the plant?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 170" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Plant Parts and Functions</text>
  <ellipse cx="160" cy="58" rx="60" ry="35" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/>
  <text x="160" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">LEAVES ★</text>
  <text x="160" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Make food (photosynthesis)</text>
  <rect x="145" y="93" width="30" height="45" fill="#92400e" rx="3"/>
  <text x="170" y="108" font-size="9" fill="#94a3b8">Stem</text>
  <text x="170" y="120" font-size="8" fill="#94a3b8">transport</text>
  <ellipse cx="160" cy="145" rx="45" ry="18" fill="#d1fae5" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="160" y="149" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Roots – absorb water</text>
  <rect x="20" y="93" width="115" height="22" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" rx="3"/>
  <text x="78" y="108" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Roots / Stem / Flowers</text>
  <text x="78" y="120" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">do NOT make food</text>
  <text x="160" y="168" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a" font-weight="bold">Leaves use sunlight + CO₂ + water → glucose + O₂</text>
</svg>

template: {CHARACTER_0} wants to make a raincoat. Which material is waterproof and would be best?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 150" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Material Properties — Raincoat</text>
  <rect x="15" y="32" width="80" height="75" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="55" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Cotton</text>
  <text x="55" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Absorbs water</text>
  <text x="55" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ NOT waterproof</text>
  <rect x="105" y="32" width="80" height="75" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="145" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Paper</text>
  <text x="145" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Gets soggy</text>
  <text x="145" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ NOT waterproof</text>
  <rect x="195" y="32" width="80" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="235" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Plastic ★</text>
  <text x="235" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Repels water</text>
  <text x="235" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">✓ Waterproof!</text>
  <rect x="285" y="32" width="80" height="75" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#cbd5e1" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="325" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Wood</text>
  <text x="325" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Absorbs water</text>
  <text x="325" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ NOT waterproof</text>
  <text x="190" y="125" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#15803d" font-weight="bold">Best material for raincoat: Plastic (waterproof)</text>
</svg>

template: What happens to water when it is heated to 100°C?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 160" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Water Heated to 100°C</text>
  <rect x="80" y="55" width="140" height="70" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="150" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#0369a1">Water (liquid)</text>
  <text x="150" y="116" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">at 100°C (boiling point)</text>
  <defs><marker id="arr6" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#7c3aed"/></marker></defs>
  <line x1="120" y1="55" x2="105" y2="30" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr6)"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="55" x2="150" y2="28" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr6)"/>
  <line x1="180" y1="55" x2="195" y2="30" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr6)"/>
  <text x="150" y="22" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#7c3aed" font-weight="bold">Water vapor (gas) ★</text>
  <rect x="20" y="50" width="55" height="22" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" rx="3"/>
  <text x="47" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Freeze ✗</text>
  <rect x="20" y="78" width="55" height="22" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" rx="3"/>
  <text x="47" y="93" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Melt ✗</text>
  <rect x="20" y="106" width="55" height="22" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" rx="3"/>
  <text x="47" y="121" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Condense ✗</text>
  <text x="150" y="148" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#7c3aed" font-weight="bold">Answer: Evaporates (turns to steam) ★</text>
</svg>

template: In the water cycle, what process changes water vapor back into liquid water?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 170" width="360" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="180" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">The Water Cycle</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrWC" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="20" y="120" width="320" height="35" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="180" y="141" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">Ocean / Lakes / Rivers</text>
  <path d="M90,120 Q80,80 60,50" fill="none" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrWC)"/>
  <text x="32" y="85" font-size="9" fill="#64748b" transform="rotate(-30,32,85)">Evaporation ↑</text>
  <path d="M170,50 Q220,30 270,45" fill="none" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/>
  <ellipse cx="130" cy="50" rx="55" ry="22" fill="#e0f2fe" stroke="#7dd3fc" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="130" y="47" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">Cloud</text>
  <text x="130" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#475569">(water vapor → droplets)</text>
  <rect x="65" y="28" width="105" height="20" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="118" y="42" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#15803d" font-weight="bold">Condensation ★</text>
  <path d="M220,72 Q245,95 250,120" fill="none" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrWC)"/>
  <text x="262" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6">Precipitation ↓</text>
  <text x="180" y="162" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Condensation: water VAPOR → liquid (cloud droplets form)</text>
</svg>

template: Which system in the human body is responsible for transporting oxygen and nutrients to all body cells?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 160" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Body Systems — Transport</text>
  <rect x="20" y="30" width="135" height="95" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="88" y="50" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Respiratory system</text>
  <text x="88" y="64" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Breathing (gas exchange)</text>
  <text x="88" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Digestive system</text>
  <text x="88" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Break down food</text>
  <text x="88" y="110" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Nervous system</text>
  <text x="88" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Control / signals</text>
  <rect x="165" y="30" width="140" height="95" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="235" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Circulatory ★</text>
  <text x="235" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Heart + Blood vessels</text>
  <text x="235" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Transports O₂ and</text>
  <text x="235" y="97" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">nutrients to ALL cells</text>
  <text x="235" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">removes CO₂ + waste</text>
  <text x="160" y="145" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Blood flows: Heart → Arteries → Capillaries → Veins → Heart</text>
</svg>

template: What is the function of the stomata in a leaf?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 160" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Stomata on Leaf Underside</text>
  <ellipse cx="160" cy="80" rx="115" ry="60" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="160" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#15803d" font-weight="bold">Leaf (underside)</text>
  <ellipse cx="130" cy="85" rx="10" ry="5" fill="#166534" stroke="#14532d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <ellipse cx="165" cy="90" rx="10" ry="5" fill="#166534" stroke="#14532d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <ellipse cx="200" cy="83" rx="10" ry="5" fill="#166534" stroke="#14532d" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <text x="160" y="108" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Stoma (pore)</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrSt" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#3b82f6"/></marker>
  <marker id="arrSt2" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#f97316"/></marker></defs>
  <line x1="40" y1="75" x2="118" y2="85" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrSt)"/>
  <text x="20" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#3b82f6">CO₂ in</text>
  <line x1="48" y1="100" x2="118" y2="92" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrSt2)"/>
  <text x="20" y="110" font-size="8" fill="#f97316">O₂ out</text>
  <text x="160" y="148" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Stomata = pores for gas exchange (CO₂ in, O₂ out) and water vapor</text>
</svg>

template: In a food chain: Grass → Grasshopper → Frog → Snake → Hawk. Which organism is the secondary consumer?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 140" width="400" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="200" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Food Chain — Trophic Levels</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrFC" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="10" y="45" width="65" height="45" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="43" y="64" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#15803d">Grass</text>
  <text x="43" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">Producer</text>
  <line x1="75" y1="67" x2="88" y2="67" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFC)"/>
  <rect x="89" y="45" width="75" height="45" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="127" y="64" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#1e40af">Grasshopper</text>
  <text x="127" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">1° Consumer</text>
  <line x1="164" y1="67" x2="177" y2="67" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFC)"/>
  <rect x="178" y="40" width="68" height="55" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="212" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#92400e">Frog ★</text>
  <text x="212" y="75" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">2° Consumer</text>
  <text x="212" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">(secondary)</text>
  <line x1="246" y1="67" x2="259" y2="67" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFC)"/>
  <rect x="260" y="45" width="60" height="45" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="290" y="64" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Snake</text>
  <text x="290" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">3° Consumer</text>
  <line x1="320" y1="67" x2="333" y2="67" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFC)"/>
  <rect x="334" y="45" width="56" height="45" fill="#ede9fe" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="362" y="64" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#7c3aed">Hawk</text>
  <text x="362" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">Apex</text>
  <text x="200" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Secondary consumer = eats the primary consumer (grasshopper)</text>
  <text x="200" y="132" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#f59e0b" font-weight="bold">Answer: Frog ★</text>
</svg>

template: Which of the following is an example of potential energy being converted to kinetic energy?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 155" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Potential Energy → Kinetic Energy</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="165" height="85" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="98" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Battery → toy car</text>
  <text x="98" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(Chemical → KE)</text>
  <text x="98" y="90" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Light bulb → light</text>
  <text x="98" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(Electrical → Light)</text>
  <rect x="200" y="35" width="165" height="85" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="283" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Rubber band ★</text>
  <text x="283" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#15803d">stretched = PE stored</text>
  <text x="283" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#15803d">released = KE (moves)</text>
  <text x="283" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Elastic PE → KE</text>
  <text x="190" y="133" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">PE (stored energy) → KE (energy of motion)</text>
  <text x="190" y="147" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#15803d" font-weight="bold">Answer: Stretched rubber band being released ★</text>
</svg>
options: A battery powering a toy car
A stretched rubber band being released
correct_answer: A stretched rubber band being released

template: {CHARACTER_0} is studying cacti and wonders why they have thorns instead of broad leaves. What is the reason?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 150" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Cactus Adaptations (Desert)</text>
  <rect x="20" y="35" width="125" height="90" fill="#fef9c3" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2" rx="6"/>
  <text x="82" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#92400e">Thorns/Spines</text>
  <text x="82" y="71" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Replace broad leaves</text>
  <text x="82" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">→ LESS surface area</text>
  <text x="82" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">→ Less water lost</text>
  <text x="82" y="113" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Also: deter animals</text>
  <rect x="155" y="35" width="125" height="90" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="218" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Main Reason ★</text>
  <text x="218" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#15803d">Reduce water loss</text>
  <text x="218" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Desert = very dry</text>
  <text x="218" y="101" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Must conserve water</text>
  <text x="150" y="138" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Thorns = reduced leaves → minimise evaporation (transpiration)</text>
</svg>

template: Which method of heat transfer does NOT require a medium (can occur in a vacuum)?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 145" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Heat Transfer — No Medium Needed</text>

  <text x="63" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">solid → solid</text>
  <text x="63" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">requires medium</text>
  <text x="63" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗</text>
  <rect x="125" y="35" width="105" height="80" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="178" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Convection</text>
  <text x="178" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">fluid movement</text>
  <text x="178" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">requires fluid</text>
  <text x="178" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗</text>
  <rect x="240" y="30" width="130" height="90" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="305" y="54" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#92400e">Radiation ★</text>
  <text x="305" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">electromagnetic</text>
  <text x="305" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">waves (e.g. sunlight)</text>
  <text x="305" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">NO medium needed</text>
  <text x="305" y="109" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">works in vacuum ✓</text>
  <text x="190" y="135" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Sun heats Earth by radiation across empty space ★</text>
</svg>

  <text x="190" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#475569">2 weights → longer stretch</text>

template: In a food chain, energy flows from:

  <text x="160" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">1° Consumer</text>
  <text x="160" y="81" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(herbivore)</text>
  <text x="160" y="94" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">eats plants</text>
  <line x1="202" y1="72" x2="218" y2="72" stroke="#f97316" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrEF)"/>

  <text x="190" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Energy flows: Producers → Consumers (one direction only) ★</text>
  <text x="190" y="132" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Each level loses ~90% energy as heat; only ~10% passed on</text>
</svg>

template: What do plants need to make food through photosynthesis?

  <defs><marker id="arrPS" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#15803d"/></marker></defs>

  <rect x="93" y="40" width="68" height="48" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>

  <rect x="171" y="40" width="80" height="48" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="211" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">CO₂</text>
  <text x="211" y="71" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Carbon dioxide</text>
  <text x="211" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#475569">(from air)</text>
  <line x1="80" y1="64" x2="91" y2="64" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="2"/>
  <line x1="161" y1="64" x2="169" y2="64" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="2"/>
  <rect x="261" y="33" width="85" height="62" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>

  <text x="304" y="69" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Glucose (food) ★</text>
  <text x="304" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">+ Oxygen</text>
  <line x1="251" y1="64" x2="259" y2="64" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrPS)"/>
  <rect x="20" y="105" width="320" height="16" fill="#fef9c3" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="180" y="117" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e" font-weight="bold">Answer: Water + Sunlight + Carbon dioxide ★</text>
  <text x="180" y="138" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂</text>
</svg>

template: What is the process called when water vapor turns into liquid water drops on a cold surface?

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 150" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Condensation</text>
  <rect x="60" y="35" width="180" height="75" fill="#e0f2fe" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#0369a1">Cold glass / surface</text>
  <text x="90" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">💨 Water vapor</text>
  <text x="90" y="93" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#475569">(warm, humid air)</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrCO" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#0369a1"/></marker></defs>
  <line x1="130" y1="82" x2="170" y2="82" stroke="#0369a1" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrCO)"/>
  <text x="210" y="75" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">💧 Liquid</text>
  <text x="210" y="89" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">water drops</text>
  <text x="150" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Condensation: water VAPOR → liquid water (cooling) ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="143" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Example: dew on glass, clouds forming</text>
</svg>

template: At what temperature does water freeze?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 140" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">States of Water — Temperature</text>
  <rect x="15" y="40" width="80" height="65" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="55" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">Ice ★</text>
  <text x="55" y="74" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1e40af">Solid</text>
  <text x="55" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#1e40af">at 0°C</text>
  <text x="55" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(or below)</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrST" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <line x1="95" y1="72" x2="108" y2="72" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrST)"/>
  <text x="108" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#f97316">heat</text>
  <rect x="109" y="40" width="80" height="65" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="149" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">Water</text>
  <text x="149" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Liquid</text>
  <text x="149" y="90" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">0°C – 100°C</text>
  <line x1="189" y1="72" x2="202" y2="72" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrST)"/>
  <rect x="203" y="40" width="80" height="65" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="243" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Steam</text>
  <text x="243" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Gas</text>
  <text x="243" y="90" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">above 100°C</text>
  <text x="150" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#1e40af" font-weight="bold">Water freezes at 0°C ★</text>
</svg>

template: Solar panels convert light energy into which form of energy?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 140" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Solar Panel — Energy Conversion</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrSP" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#475569"/></marker></defs>
  <circle cx="55" cy="65" r="28" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="55" y="60" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">☀️ Sun</text>
  <text x="55" y="74" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Light energy</text>
  <line x1="83" y1="65" x2="115" y2="65" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrSP)"/>
  <rect x="116" y="42" width="85" height="46" fill="#1e40af" stroke="#1e3a8a" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="159" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="white">Solar Panel</text>
  <text x="159" y="77" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#bfdbfe">(photovoltaic)</text>
  <line x1="201" y1="65" x2="233" y2="65" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrSP)"/>
  <rect x="234" y="45" width="55" height="40" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="3"/>
  <text x="262" y="63" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">⚡</text>
  <text x="262" y="76" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">Electricity ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="110" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#15803d" font-weight="bold">Light energy → Electrical energy ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="126" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Solar panels contain photovoltaic cells that convert light → electricity</text>
</svg>

template: Which of the following is NOT a renewable source of energy?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 140" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Renewable vs Non-Renewable Energy</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="50" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22">☀️</text>
  <text x="50" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Solar</text>
  <text x="50" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">Renewable ✓</text>
  <rect x="100" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="140" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22">💨</text>
  <text x="140" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Wind</text>
  <text x="140" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">Renewable ✓</text>
  <rect x="190" y="30" width="90" height="80" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="235" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22">🪨</text>
  <text x="235" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Coal ★</text>
  <text x="235" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">NOT renewable!</text>
  <text x="235" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#475569">fossil fuel, finite</text>
  <rect x="290" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="330" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="15">💧</text>
  <text x="330" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Hydro-</text>
  <text x="330" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">electric</text>
  <text x="330" y="91" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">Renewable ✓</text>
  <text x="190" y="124" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Coal = fossil fuel → will run out → non-renewable ★</text>
</svg>

template: {CHARACTER_0} pushes a toy car on different surfaces. On which surface will the car travel the farthest?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 140" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Friction on Different Surfaces</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="80" height="75" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="50" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Rough carpet</text>
  <text x="50" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20">🪨</text>
  <text x="50" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">High friction</text>
  <text x="50" y="107" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">car stops soon</text>
  <rect x="100" y="30" width="95" height="85" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="148" y="53" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Smooth tile ★</text>
  <text x="148" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20">🚗</text>
  <text x="148" y="90" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">LEAST friction</text>
  <text x="148" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">travels FARTHEST!</text>
  <rect x="203" y="35" width="80" height="75" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="243" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Grass</text>
  <text x="243" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20">🌿</text>
  <text x="243" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">Medium friction</text>
  <rect x="293" y="35" width="80" height="75" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="333" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Sand</text>
  <text x="333" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20">🏖</text>
  <text x="333" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">High friction</text>
  <text x="190" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Less friction = more distance traveled with same force ★</text>
</svg>

template: What force pulls objects toward the center of the Earth?

  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Gravitational Force</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrGF" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L6,3 L0,6 Z" fill="#3b82f6"/></marker></defs>

  <line x1="150" y1="82" x2="150" y2="110" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="3" marker-end="url(#arrGF)"/>

  <ellipse cx="150" cy="128" rx="70" ry="12" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2"/>

  <text x="150" y="22" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Gravity pulls all objects toward Earth's center ★</text>
</svg>

template: What happens when you connect batteries in series in a circuit?

</svg>

template: {CHARACTER_0} is making a simple circuit. Which material should {CHARACTER_0} use to connect the battery to the bulb?
diagram:

  <text x="180" y="124" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Copper wire connects battery to bulb — metal conducts electricity ★</text>
</svg>

template: Which change is reversible?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 140" width="360" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="180" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Reversible vs Irreversible Changes</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="160" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="90" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Reversible ★</text>
  <text x="90" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Melting ice → water</text>
  <text x="90" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Dissolving sugar</text>
  <text x="90" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Bending wire</text>
  <text x="90" y="109" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(can be undone)</text>
  <rect x="185" y="35" width="160" height="80" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="265" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Irreversible</text>
  <text x="265" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Burning paper</text>
  <text x="265" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Cooking eggs</text>
  <text x="265" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Rusting</text>
  <text x="265" y="109" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(cannot be undone)</text>
  <text x="180" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Melting ice = reversible change ★ (cool it → ice again)</text>
</svg>

template: What is the process of changing a solid directly to a gas called?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 155" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">States of Matter — Change of State</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrSM" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#475569"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="20" y="40" width="80" height="40" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="60" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#1e40af">SOLID</text>
  <line x1="100" y1="60" x2="135" y2="60" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrSM)"/>
  <text x="118" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#f97316">heat</text>
  <rect x="136" y="40" width="80" height="40" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="176" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#0369a1">LIQUID</text>
  <line x1="216" y1="60" x2="251" y2="60" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrSM)"/>
  <text x="234" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#f97316">heat</text>
  <rect x="252" y="40" width="75" height="40" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="290" y="65" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#475569">GAS</text>
  <path d="M60,80 Q60,125 290,125 L290,80" fill="none" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="6,3" marker-end="url(#arrSM)"/>
  <text x="170" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#7c3aed">Sublimation ★</text>
  <text x="170" y="131" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#7c3aed">Solid → Gas (skips liquid!)</text>
  <text x="170" y="148" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Example: dry ice (solid CO₂) turns directly to gas ★</text>
</svg>

template: Which type of surface reflects light the best?

  <rect x="206" y="35" width="80" height="70" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="246" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">White rough</text>

  <text x="85" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#1e40af">Cold water 🧊</text>
  <text x="85" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Molecules move slowly</text>
  <text x="85" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1e40af">Salt dissolves slowly</text>
  <text x="85" y="97" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">slower rate ✗</text>
  <rect x="180" y="28" width="150" height="90" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="255" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">Hot water ☀️ ★</text>
  <text x="255" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Molecules move fast</text>
  <text x="255" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">More collisions</text>
  <text x="255" y="93" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Salt dissolves FASTER</text>
  <text x="255" y="107" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">faster rate ✓</text>
  <text x="170" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Higher temperature → molecules move faster → dissolves faster ★</text>
</svg>

Salt dissolves faster in hot water

template: Which of the following is an example of a physical change?
diagram:

  <text x="90" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Physical Change ★</text>
  <text x="90" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Boiling water ★</text>
  <text x="90" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Melting ice</text>
  <text x="90" y="96" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Cutting paper</text>
  <text x="90" y="109" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(NO new substance)</text>
  <rect x="183" y="35" width="162" height="80" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="264" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Chemical Change</text>
  <text x="264" y="70" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Burning wood</text>
  <text x="264" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Cooking egg</text>

  <text x="264" y="109" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(NEW substance formed)</text>
  <text x="180" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Boiling water = physical change (water→steam, no new substance) ★</text>
</svg>

template: {CHARACTER_0} wants to separate a mixture of sand and salt. What is the best method?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 150" width="360" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="180" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Separating Sand and Salt</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrSP" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="85" height="55" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="58" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">Sand + Salt</text>
  <text x="58" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">mixture</text>
  <line x1="100" y1="62" x2="115" y2="62" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrSP)"/>
  <text x="103" y="54" font-size="7" fill="#64748b">Step 1</text>
  <rect x="116" y="35" width="70" height="55" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="151" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#0369a1">Dissolve</text>
  <text x="151" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#0369a1">in water</text>
  <text x="151" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(salt dissolves)</text>
  <line x1="186" y1="62" x2="201" y2="62" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrSP)"/>
  <text x="186" y="54" font-size="7" fill="#64748b">Step 2</text>
  <rect x="202" y="35" width="65" height="55" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="234" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">Filter</text>
  <text x="234" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">(sand stays</text>
  <text x="234" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">on filter)</text>
  <line x1="267" y1="62" x2="282" y2="62" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrSP)"/>
  <text x="264" y="54" font-size="7" fill="#64748b">Step 3</text>
  <rect x="283" y="35" width="65" height="55" fill="#fce7f3" stroke="#ec4899" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="315" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#be185d">Evaporate</text>
  <text x="315" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#be185d">water →</text>
  <text x="315" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#be185d">salt left</text>
  <text x="180" y="108" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">Dissolve → Filter (remove sand) → Evaporate (get salt) ★</text>
</svg>
options: Dissolve in water, filter, then evaporate
correct_answer: Dissolve in water, filter, then evaporate

template: What would happen if all the plants in an ecosystem disappeared?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 140" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">If All Plants Disappear</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="130" height="80" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>

  <text x="80" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">No food for</text>
  <text x="80" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">herbivores</text>
  <text x="80" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">→ herbivores die</text>
  <text x="80" y="112" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">→ carnivores die</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrEC" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#dc2626"/></marker></defs>
  <line x1="145" y1="75" x2="162" y2="75" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrEC)"/>
  <rect x="163" y="35" width="142" height="80" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="234" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">Ecosystem</text>
  <text x="234" y="71" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">collapses! ★</text>
  <text x="234" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">All animals lose</text>
  <text x="234" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">their food source</text>
  <text x="160" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Plants = base of ALL food chains → ecosystem collapses ★</text>
</svg>
options: Animals would find other food sources easily
The entire food chain would collapse
correct_answer: The entire food chain would collapse

template: {CHARACTER_0} observes that a balloon filled with air shrinks when placed in a refrigerator. This is because:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 140" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Balloon in Fridge — Why It Shrinks</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="130" height="80" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">Room temperature</text>
  <ellipse cx="80" cy="88" rx="38" ry="25" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="80" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">balloon</text>
  <text x="80" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">LARGE</text>
  <rect x="175" y="28" width="130" height="90" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="240" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">In fridge ★</text>
  <text x="240" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1e40af">Air cools → molecules</text>
  <text x="240" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1e40af">move slower</text>
  <text x="240" y="93" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1e40af">→ less pressure</text>
  <ellipse cx="240" cy="109" rx="22" ry="14" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="240" y="113" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">shrinks ★</text>
  <text x="160" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Cold → air molecules slow → take less space → balloon shrinks ★</text>
</svg>
options: The air particles inside move slower and take up less space
correct_answer: The air particles inside move slower and take up less space

  <text x="83" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Pressure: HIGH</text>
  <text x="83" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Water boils at 100°C</text>
  <text x="83" y="103" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(normal)</text>
  <rect x="170" y="28" width="135" height="90" fill="#fce7f3" stroke="#ec4899" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="238" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#be185d">On mountain ★</text>
  <text x="238" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#be185d">Pressure: LOWER</text>

template: Which of the following is an example of energy conservation?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 140" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Conservation of Energy</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="145" height="80" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Correct ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Pendulum slows down</text>
  <text x="88" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">due to friction + air</text>
  <text x="88" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">KE → heat/sound</text>
  <text x="88" y="110" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(energy conserved ★)</text>
  <rect x="175" y="35" width="155" height="80" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="253" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Incorrect ideas</text>
  <text x="253" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ Energy created</text>
  <text x="253" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ Energy destroyed</text>
  <text x="253" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ Forever same form</text>
  <text x="170" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Energy transforms but is never created or destroyed ★</text>
</svg>
options: A swinging pendulum gradually slows down
A falling ball speeds up indefinitely
correct_answer: A swinging pendulum gradually slows down

  <text x="60" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">features</text>

template: Which human activity contributes MOST to deforestation?

template: If all producers in a food web were removed, what would happen?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 145" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Food Web — Producers Removed</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrFW" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="15" y="60" width="70" height="25" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="50" y="77" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#dc2626">🌿 Plants ★</text>
  <text x="50" y="95" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">ALL removed</text>
  <line x1="85" y1="72" x2="103" y2="62" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFW)"/>
  <rect x="104" y="50" width="80" height="25" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="144" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">🐰 Rabbit → ✗ dies</text>
  <line x1="184" y1="62" x2="202" y2="62" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFW)"/>
  <rect x="203" y="50" width="80" height="25" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="243" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">🦊 Fox → ✗ dies</text>
  <line x1="283" y1="62" x2="301" y2="62" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrFW)"/>
  <rect x="302" y="50" width="35" height="25" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="320" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">all die</text>
  <rect x="70" y="100" width="200" height="22" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="170" y="115" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626" font-weight="bold">All consumers eventually die ★</text>
  <text x="170" y="138" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Producers (plants) removed → entire food chain collapses ★</text>
</svg>

template: A nail is attracted to a magnet. What can we conclude?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 135" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Nail Attracted to Magnet — Why?</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrMG" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="30" y="40" width="100" height="55" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="80" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">🧲 Magnet</text>
  <text x="80" y="77" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">magnetic force</text>
  <line x1="130" y1="67" x2="163" y2="67" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrMG)"/>
  <text x="147" y="62" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">attracts</text>
  <rect x="164" y="40" width="106" height="55" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="217" y="62" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">🔩 Steel nail</text>
  <text x="217" y="76" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">contains IRON ★</text>
  <text x="217" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(magnetic material)</text>
  <text x="150" y="114" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Only iron/steel/nickel are magnetic → nail contains iron ★</text>
</svg>
options: The nail is made of plastic
The nail contains iron or steel
The nail is made of copper
correct_answer: The nail contains iron or steel

template: {CHARACTER_0} wants to make ice cream freeze faster. What should {CHARACTER_0} do?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 135" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Ice Cream Freezes Faster with Salt</text>
  <rect x="25" y="35" width="115" height="75" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="83" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Plain ice</text>
  <text x="83" y="71" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Freezes at 0°C</text>
  <text x="83" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">Normal rate</text>
  <text x="83" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">slow for ice cream</text>
  <rect x="160" y="28" width="120" height="88" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="220" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Ice + Salt ★</text>
  <text x="220" y="66" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Lowers freezing</text>
  <text x="220" y="79" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">point below 0°C</text>
  <text x="220" y="93" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Gets COLDER!</text>
  <text x="220" y="107" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">→ freezes faster ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="124" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Salt lowers freezing point → ice gets colder → faster freezing ★</text>
</svg>
options: Add salt to the ice around the container
Add sugar to the ice cream
Put it in a warmer place
correct_answer: Add salt to the ice around the container

template: Which of the following shows the correct sequence from smallest to largest level of organization?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 135" width="360" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="180" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Levels of Organisation in Living Things</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrLO" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="10" y="45" width="70" height="50" fill="#fce7f3" stroke="#ec4899" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="45" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#be185d">Cell</text>
  <text x="45" y="81" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">smallest</text>
  <line x1="80" y1="70" x2="98" y2="70" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrLO)"/>
  <rect x="99" y="45" width="70" height="50" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="134" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">Tissue</text>
  <text x="134" y="81" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">similar cells</text>
  <line x1="169" y1="70" x2="187" y2="70" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrLO)"/>
  <rect x="188" y="45" width="70" height="50" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="223" y="68" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Organ</text>
  <text x="223" y="81" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(e.g. heart)</text>
  <line x1="258" y1="70" x2="276" y2="70" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrLO)"/>
  <rect x="277" y="38" width="78" height="64" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="316" y="63" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#92400e">System ★</text>
  <text x="316" y="76" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">(e.g. circulatory,</text>
  <text x="316" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">digestive)</text>
  <text x="180" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Cell → Tissue → Organ → Organ System ★</text>
</svg>

template: What is excretion?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 135" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Life Process: Excretion</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="130" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Excretion ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Removing METABOLIC</text>
  <text x="80" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">waste products</text>
  <text x="80" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">(CO₂, urea, sweat)</text>
  <rect x="160" y="35" width="145" height="75" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="233" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">NOT excretion:</text>
  <text x="233" y="71" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">✗ Egestion (feces)</text>
  <text x="233" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">✗ Indigestion (digestion)</text>
  <text x="233" y="97" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#64748b">(undigested food)</text>
  <text x="160" y="124" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Excretion = removing metabolic waste (made by cells) ★</text>
</svg>
options: Removing waste products from the body
correct_answer: Removing waste products from the body

template: Animals are classified into vertebrates and invertebrates based on:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 135" width="360" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="180" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Vertebrates vs Invertebrates</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="155" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="93" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Vertebrates ★</text>
  <text x="93" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">HAVE backbone</text>
  <text x="93" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🐟🐸🦎🐦🐭</text>
  <text x="93" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">fish, amphibian, reptile, bird, mammal</text>
  <rect x="185" y="35" width="165" height="75" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="268" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#92400e">Invertebrates</text>
  <text x="268" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">NO backbone</text>
  <text x="268" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">🦋🕷️🐛🪱🦐</text>
  <text x="268" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">insect, spider, worm, crustacean</text>
  <text x="180" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Vertebrates = animals WITH a backbone (spine) ★</text>
</svg>

template: {CHARACTER_0} places a potted plant in a dark room for several days. What life process will be affected first?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 135" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Plant Kept in Dark Room</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="145" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="88" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">With sunlight ★</text>
  <text x="88" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Photosynthesis ✓</text>
  <text x="88" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Food produced</text>
  <text x="88" y="101" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">green, healthy</text>
  <rect x="175" y="35" width="155" height="75" fill="#334155" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="253" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="white">In dark room ★</text>
  <text x="253" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#fde68a">Photosynthesis stops</text>
  <text x="253" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#fde68a">No food made</text>
  <text x="253" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#fde68a">yellow/wilts</text>
  <text x="170" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Photosynthesis is FIRST affected (needs sunlight) ★</text>
</svg>

template: In a forest ecosystem, which organisms return nutrients to the soil?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 135" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Decomposers Return Nutrients to Soil</text>
  <defs><marker id="arrDN" markerWidth="5" markerHeight="5" refX="3" refY="3" orient="auto"><path d="M0,0 L5,3 L0,5 Z" fill="#64748b"/></marker></defs>
  <rect x="15" y="38" width="90" height="35" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="60" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Dead organisms</text>
  <line x1="105" y1="55" x2="133" y2="55" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrDN)"/>
  <rect x="134" y="38" width="85" height="35" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="1.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="177" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">🍄 Decomposers</text>
  <text x="177" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">(bacteria, fungi)</text>
  <line x1="219" y1="55" x2="247" y2="55" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrDN)"/>
  <rect x="248" y="38" width="80" height="35" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="4"/>
  <text x="288" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Nutrients ★</text>
  <text x="288" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">returned to soil</text>
  <rect x="95" y="95" width="160" height="22" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="175" y="110" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">🌱 Plants absorb nutrients → grow</text>
  <text x="170" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Decomposers recycle nutrients back into soil for plants ★</text>
</svg>

template: Which state of matter has a definite shape and volume?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 360 135" width="360" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="180" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">States of Matter — Properties</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="100" height="75" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="60" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e40af">Solid ★</text>
  <text x="60" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1e40af">Fixed shape</text>
  <text x="60" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#1e40af">Fixed volume</text>
  <text x="60" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">particles vibrate</text>
  <rect x="120" y="35" width="100" height="75" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="170" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#0369a1">Liquid</text>
  <text x="170" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">No fixed shape</text>
  <text x="170" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Fixed volume</text>
  <text x="170" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">flows/pours</text>
  <rect x="230" y="35" width="120" height="75" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="290" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#475569">Gas</text>
  <text x="290" y="73" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">No fixed shape</text>
  <text x="290" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#475569">No fixed volume</text>
  <text x="290" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">fills container</text>
  <text x="180" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Solid = definite shape + volume, particles closely packed ★</text>
</svg>

template: {CHARACTER_0} wants to test if a material floats or sinks. What property is {CHARACTER_0} testing?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 135" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Float or Sink? — Density</text>
  <rect x="40" y="38" width="240" height="60" fill="#bae6fd" stroke="#0ea5e9" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>
  <text x="160" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#0369a1">Water</text>
  <rect x="65" y="52" width="40" height="35" fill="#fde68a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2" rx="3"/>
  <text x="85" y="74" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#92400e">Wood</text>
  <text x="85" y="82" text-anchor="middle" font-size="7" fill="#15803d">floats ↑</text>
  <rect x="130" y="68" width="40" height="24" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2" rx="2"/>
  <text x="150" y="84" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="white">Iron</text>
  <rect x="200" y="60" width="35" height="28" fill="#fce7f3" stroke="#ec4899" stroke-width="2" rx="2"/>
  <text x="218" y="79" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#be185d">Plastic</text>
  <text x="85" y="108" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#15803d">less dense than water</text>
  <text x="150" y="108" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">sinks ↓</text>
  <text x="218" y="99" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">can float</text>
  <text x="160" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Float/sink depends on density compared to water ★</text>
</svg>

template: Why are metals used for making cooking pots?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 320 130" width="320" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="160" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Why Pots are Made of Metal</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="130" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="80" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Metal ★</text>
  <text x="80" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Good heat conductor</text>
  <text x="80" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Heat transfers quickly</text>
  <text x="80" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#64748b">✓ cooks food efficiently</text>
  <rect x="165" y="35" width="140" height="70" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="235" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Wood / Plastic</text>
  <text x="235" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Poor conductors</text>
  <text x="235" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">(insulators)</text>
  <text x="235" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">✗ heat unevenly / melt</text>
  <text x="160" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Metals are good heat conductors → ideal for cooking pots ★</text>
</svg>
options: They are good conductors of heat
correct_answer: They are good conductors of heat

template: Which material would be best for making electrical wires?
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="150" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Why Copper Wires are Used</text>
  <rect x="20" y="35" width="260" height="70" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2" rx="6"/>
  <text x="150" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#92400e">Copper ★</text>
  <text x="150" y="74" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">✓ Excellent electrical conductor</text>
  <text x="150" y="87" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">✓ Flexible (easy to bend into wire)</text>
  <text x="150" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#92400e">✓ Relatively affordable</text>
  <text x="150" y="118" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Copper: best electrical conductor + flexible → used for wires ★</text>
</svg>

template: Steel is attracted to magnets, but aluminum is not. This shows that:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 380 135" width="380" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="190" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Magnetic vs Non-Magnetic Metals</text>
  <rect x="10" y="35" width="75" height="70" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/>

template: According to the law of conservation of energy:
diagram:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 340 130" width="340" font-family="Arial,sans-serif">
  <text x="170" y="16" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Conservation of Energy</text>
  <rect x="15" y="35" width="150" height="75" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="90" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-weight="bold" fill="#15803d">Correct ★</text>
  <text x="90" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">Energy transforms</text>
  <text x="90" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">from one form to</text>
  <text x="90" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#15803d">another ★</text>
  <rect x="180" y="35" width="150" height="75" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5" rx="6"/>
  <text x="255" y="57" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Wrong!</text>
  <text x="255" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ Created</text>
  <text x="255" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ Destroyed</text>
  <text x="255" y="98" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">✗ Disappears</text>
  <text x="170" y="120" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed ★</text>
</svg>
options: Energy can be created from nothing
Energy can be transformed but not created or destroyed
Energy always stays the same form
correct_answer: Energy can be transformed but not created or destroyed

template: {CHARACTER_0} touches a metal spoon and wooden spoon left in hot soup. The metal spoon feels hotter because:
